references.title: Complexity-based attentive interactive student performance prediction for personalized course study planning

extracted.Students:
43 highschool students in Dataset 1,

501 students of undisclosed level for Dataset 2
extracted.Courses:
5 different courses in dataset 1 

8 Courses in dataset 2
extracted.Data sources:
Student Grades in Different Tests

Student Information
extracted.Features: [Factorization Machines Features, DNN Features, ANN Features]
extracted.Preprocessing Details:
Authors use two datasets with different feature sets. 

Dataset 2 includes information about the concept of complexity, something not observable in the K-12 dataset.
extracted.Models:
Factorization Machines,
Neural Factorization Machines,
Attentional Factorization Machines, 
Deep Factorization Machines,
CAISP
extracted.Comments:
CAISP is a model that works in 3 stages:

1. Feature Learning - The creation of features using 3 approaches: DNN Features, Factorization Machines Features and Attention Features

2. Feature Fusion

3. Prediction - The feature set fused in this way is given as input to a logistic regression
extracted.Limitations:
Undisclosed Information about second Dataset. 

Behavioral Features are not Considered, only prior performance.

annotations.text: Student performance prediction is used to leverage existing information about students to predict future learning performance. Authors follow a predictive approach to predict course grades. Most studies look into different aspects of learning but fail to account for how different factors (features) affect different learners.

Authors propose Complexity-based Attentive Interactive Student Performance (CAISP). The model uses attention networks. When tested on 2 real-word, datasets, CAISP outperformed two state-of-the-art solutions.
